name: Claude Hopkins
abstract: Claude Driskett Hopkins (August 24, 1903 – February 19, 1984) was an American jazz stride pianist and bandleader.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: If they’ve got Kid Ory and Barney Bigard playing and Claude Hopkins for Christ’s sake playing on those Ma Rainey records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: In August I was contacted to do a two-weeks tour with Claude Hopkins, the pianist who had been quite a popular bandleader... Those that I remember on the tour were Ernest "Bass" Hill, Joe Jordon... Joe Hayman... Norman Thronton and Lem Johnson...

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: So I got Jo Jones, Claude Hopkins, Budd Johnson, Benny Morton, Roy Eldridge, and I mentioned Jo Jones and myself. You couldn’t get a more senior group than that. So we were going to go to California to do this concert.
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: I stayed with the group because I did a recording date with Claude Hopkins. He did my tune, for one thing, 'Yacht Club Swing.' ... I had Claude Hopkins. Claude Hopkins would go on piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: I only worked with Claude Hopkins in Atlantic City and early, early '20's at the -- place called the Bellman's Inn. I used to play with him down there.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: I played up there. Played up there. I doubled up there (unintelligible) played with Claude up there, Claude Hopkins and a fellow named Eddie W____
